hub_subtitle: From hesitant communicator to confident global business leader

lesson.menu_desc: A checkpoint over a celebratory business lunch with Karen: recap the whole journey from Lessons 9-15 and put it all together — small talk, problem solving (conditionals), cultural intelligence (modals), polite indirect questions, future plans (will / going to) and reporting back (reported speech) — while you host a meal, recommend a dish, raise a toast, and reflect out loud on how far your English has come — 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 — Review & Consolidation — The Business Lunch
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Milton Sayegh | Aula 16 — Review & Consolidation | Business English for Luxury Export | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Hi Milton, it is Karen. I am so glad we finally found a date for lunch. I booked us a table at that little Italian place near my office for one o'clock on Thursday. I thought it would be nice to celebrate properly, because honestly this past year working with you has been a real milestone for us. When you have a moment, let me know if there is anything you cannot eat, and I will tell the restaurant. Oh, and bring your appetite, because they say the appetizers there are the best part. I would love to just relax, catch up, and recap everything we have built together, no agenda, no slides. See you Thursday, and thank you again for everything. It really means a lot.

Hey, this is Milton, just a quick voice note to myself after lunch with Karen. A year ago I would have dreaded a meal like this in English. I would have rehearsed every line and still frozen. Today I hosted it. I recommended the appetizers, I raised a toast, and we talked for two hours without me reaching for Portuguese once. Karen told me she was impressed, and she said the whole partnership felt easier now that we understood each other so well. The biggest takeaway for me is confidence. I can solve a problem on the phone, ask an indirect question politely, talk about what we are going to do next quarter, and report a conversation back to my team, all without panic. If a client calls tomorrow with bad news, I know exactly what I would say. This was the milestone I needed. The English is not the goal anymore. It is just the tool, and finally it feels like mine.
